messages.content: How do I select all rows from a table called users?
Use SELECT * FROM users; to retrieve all rows and columns from the users table.
How do I filter rows where age is greater than 30?
Add a WHERE clause: SELECT * FROM users WHERE age > 30;
How do I count the total number of rows in a table?
Use the COUNT function: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users;
How do I get only unique values from a column?
Use DISTINCT: SELECT DISTINCT country FROM users;
How do I sort results by a column in descending order?
Use ORDER BY with DESC: SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY created_at DESC;
How do I join two tables on a common column?
Use an INNER JOIN: SELECT u.name, o.total FROM users u INNER JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id;
How do I insert a new row into a table?
Use INSERT INTO: INSERT INTO users (name, email, age) VALUES ('Alice', 'alice@example.com', 28);
How do I update an existing row?
Use UPDATE with a WHERE clause: UPDATE users SET email = 'new@example.com' WHERE id = 42; Always include WHERE to avoid updating every row.
How do I delete a row from a table?
Use DELETE with a WHERE clause: DELETE FROM users WHERE id = 42; Be careful — omitting WHERE deletes all rows.
How do I group rows and compute an aggregate per group?
Use GROUP BY: SELECT country, COUNT(*) as total FROM users GROUP BY country; This counts users per country.
How do I limit the number of rows returned?
Use LIMIT: SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10; This returns the 10 most recent users.
What is the difference between WHERE and HAVING?
WHERE filters rows before grouping; HAVING filters groups after aggregation. Example: SELECT country, COUNT(*) as total FROM users GROUP BY country HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;
How do I find rows where a column value is NULL?
Use IS NULL: SELECT * FROM users WHERE phone IS NULL; You cannot use = NULL — it will always return no results.
How do I create an index to speed up queries?
Use CREATE INDEX: CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email); This speeds up queries that filter or sort by the email column.
How do I add a new column to an existing table?
Use ALTER TABLE: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN phone VARCHAR(20); The new column will be NULL for all existing rows unless you specify a DEFAULT.
